channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: Israel says it will open direct talks with Lebanon but not halt attacks on Hezbollah
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced that his country was ready for direct negotiations amid international calls for Israel to stop its bombing in Lebanon.

item.title: Emperor penguins have just been declared endangered
item.description: The Antarctic fur seal also was newly labeled as endangered by a key international group, which cited a warming world.

item.title: Countless Americans face a scary retirement. This rule could change that.
item.description: When it comes to retirement investing, the deck is stacked against most workers.

item.title: A statue for this animal in today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Thursday points.

item.title: The Supreme Court remade by Trump has ushered in historic defeats for civil rights
item.description: The court is the first since at least the ’50s to reject claims in a majority of cases involving women and minorities, an analysis conducted for The Post shows.

item.title: Iran ceasefire. Covid vaccines. Pam Bondi. Gilgo Beach suspect. Supreme Court. Social media detox. Artemis II. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: The Iran war’s exit ramp has become a diplomatic maze
item.description: The war began without a strategy for victory. The peace has no formula either.

item.title: 7 walkable U.S. cities where you don’t need a car to have a great vacation
item.description: These fun, picturesque urban centers invite visitors to explore on foot.

item.title: Iran accuses U.S. and Israel of ceasefire violations, as traffic remains stuck in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran might withdraw from talks if violations of the ceasefire continued, Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said in a statement Wednesday, which detailed apparent breaches of the agreement, including Israeli attacks in Lebanon, a drone intercepted in Iran and what he said was President Donald Trump’s denial of Iran’s right to enrich uranium.

item.title: A new study shows simple ways to avoid dementia
item.description: Keeping mentally engaged can reduce dementia risk, especially among older adults.

item.title: As you age, eating these 7 nutrients becomes even more important
item.description: There are certain vitamins, minerals and macronutrients that everyone needs to pay more attention to after they reach their 50s and 60s.

item.title: ‘Ketamine Queen’ sentenced to 15 years over Matthew Perry’s death
item.description: Jasveen Sangha pleaded guilty in September to drug-related charges, including providing the actor with the ketamine that killed him.

item.title: Pam Bondi won’t testify before House about Epstein files, Department of Justice says
item.description: Some House members say they still intend to pursue their subpoena of the former attorney general, despite her firing last week.

item.title: I’m an American living in Europe. It’s leaving the U.S. — fast.
item.description: Europeans are hedging against coercion in security, trade, education and everyday life.

item.title: They go halfsies, but, surprise! Husband has millions. Hax readers give advice.
item.description: Her mom taught her to insist on maintaining separate finances. So her husband, separately, got himself very rich.

item.title: If you aren’t losing weight with GLP-1 drugs, this may be one reason why
item.description: Two genes involved in gut hormones that influence appetite and digestion may be a factor in how people respond to the weight loss drugs, according to a new study.

item.title: How do restaurants decide which unlucky diner gets stuck next to the bathroom? Our food critic is answering questions.
item.description: Post food critic Elazar Sontag entertains your restaurant questions, rants and raves.

item.title: Trump’s apocalyptic Iran threat paused war but fueled debate about his credibility, morality
item.description: The president’s threat to wipe out “a whole civilization” raised the possibility that he would go even beyond his earlier warning to attack civilian infrastructure, which violates the laws of war. His post set off nuclear panic and drew condemnation across the political spectrum.

item.title: Iran ceasefire. Pete Hegseth. Election results. Artemis photos. Kidnapped journalist. Rising prices. Hero rat. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Democratic-backed candidate wins Wisconsin Supreme Court election, expanding liberals’ majority in the swing state
item.description: Voters gave Chris Taylor a 10-year term on the state Supreme Court, the Associated Press projected, widening the liberal majority from 4-3 to 5-2. Taylor’s victory over conservative Maria Lazar, a fellow judge on the Wisconsin Court of Appeals, positions liberals to keep their majority until at least 2030.

item.title: Trump-endorsed Republican Clay Fuller wins special election runoff to fill House seat previously held by Marjorie Taylor Greene
item.description: Fuller, a district attorney, defeated Democrat Shawn Harris in northwest Georgia. He will serve out the remainder of Greene’s term, which ends in January.

item.title: Heat pump water heaters are the future. Here’s what to know, according to our columnist.
item.description: Michael J. Coren shared tips on what to buy, how to save and what to avoid before plugging in a heat pump water heater.

item.title: Trump suspends strikes on Iran for two weeks, backing down on threat of widespread destruction
item.description: Just 90 minutes before President Donald Trump’s 8 p.m. deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz or face massive strikes on its energy infrastructure and bridges, the president granted a two-week extension for diplomacy to continue. The postponement followed an appeal from Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, whose government has been serving as a mediator between Iran and the U.S.

item.title: Our travel reporter tried to get into Berlin’s most famous nightclubs. It was humbling.
item.description: Natalie B. Compton attempted to navigate the city’s iconic (and pants-optional) club scene as a tourist. Here’s what she learned in her effort to experience Berghain, KitKat and Tresor.

item.title: Who will replace Marjorie Taylor Greene? In Georgia’s 14th, I saw clues.
item.description: Democrats’ national focus on No Kings misses what local voters want from their government.

item.title: Hegseth’s clashes with Army Secretary Dan Driscoll spur officials to ask if they can coexist. Driscoll says he’s not leaving.
item.description: Driscoll, praised by the White House for his efforts during the Iran war, said in a statement to The Washington Post that “I have no plans to depart or resign as the Secretary of the Army.”

item.title: U.S. journalist kidnapped by Iran-backed militia is released in prisoner swap with Iraq
item.description: Shelly Kittleson, a freelancer for several U.S. outlets, was seized last week in Baghdad by Kataib Hezbollah, a Shiite militia aligned with Iran.

item.title: Hegseth’s rosy claims about Iran war contradict reality, raising risk of misinforming the public and the president, officials say
item.description: “Pete is not speaking truth to the president,” one administration official said of Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth. “As a result, the president is out there repeating misleading information.”

item.title: Carolyn Hax: She has cancer. Is it too grim to host one last big party?
item.description: Carolyn Hax: She has cancer. Is it too grim to host one last big party?

item.title: What spending probes at DHS reveal about Kristi Noem’s time in office
item.description: Multiple investigations are examining potential wrongdoing under Noem’s leadership. A Washington Post review of documents offers a rare inside window on the power wielded by Noem’s top aide, Corey Lewandowski, and a handful of advisers.

item.title: The ripple effects of NASA’s Artemis mission could be bigger than you think
item.description: The moon and Mars could create a new human species.

item.title: A Sunday speech in today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Tuesday points.

item.title: Trump warns ‘a whole civilization will die tonight’ if Iran doesn’t make a deal
item.description: The president had issued a deadline of 8 p.m. Eastern time for Iran to open up the Strait of Hormuz, pledging destruction by midnight if leaders don’t comply.

item.title: Iran deadline. Moon mission. Georgia election. Steve Bannon. El Niño. March Madness. Relax rows. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Trump renews threat to destroy Iranian bridges and power plants if Strait of Hormuz is not reopened by Tuesday night
item.description: The president’s warning came as he briefed reporters at the White House on the daring mission to save two U.S. airmen whose fighter jet was shot down over Iran on Friday. He brushed off a question about whether bombing such infrastructure would violate the Geneva Conventions.

item.title: ICE arrests in D.C. region reach nearly 20,000 during Trump’s second term
item.description: Of the roughly 19,500 people arrested by Immigration and Customs Enforcement in D.C., Maryland and Virginia between Jan. 20, 2025, and March 10, 2026, about 11,600 — 60 percent — had no prior criminal record.

item.title: The Artemis II crew has flown farther from Earth than any humans before
item.description: The crew of the NASA mission has zoomed more than 248,655 miles from Earth in a historic voyage around the far side of the moon. The new distance record, set about 2 p.m. Eastern on Monday, surpasses a milestone set by Apollo 13 in 1970.

item.title: Virginians deeply divided on Spanberger months after her landslide win, poll finds
item.description: Democrat Abigail Spanberger won the race for Virginia governor by 15 points last year after touting her reputation for bipartisanship built on three terms representing a conservative-leaning district in Congress. But her approval rating stands at 47 percent two months into her term, a Washington Post-Schar School poll finds.

item.title: Supreme Court sides with Steve Bannon in efforts to dismiss his conviction for contempt of Congress
item.description: The former chief strategist to President Donald Trump spent four months in prison in 2024 after defying a congressional subpoena related to the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol. The justices vacated a judgment by an appeals court that upheld his conviction, sending the case back for reconsideration.

item.title: Schools are closing for the NFL draft. What does this teach students?
item.description: Pittsburgh prides itself on showing up and working hard. Students should, too.

item.title: He spent years working for Elon Musk. Here’s what this former Tesla president learned.
item.description: The billionaire’s demanding leadership style shaped one of the world’s most valuable companies. Jon McNeill shares how he survived.

item.title: Trump’s threat. Iran rescue. Moon mission. Secretive deals. March Madness. Dementia risk. Fart science. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: How Congress could put the Constitution in every American’s pocket
item.description: The Liberty Dollar Bill Act would be a step toward national unity, Randy Wright writes.

item.title: Surprising links between autism, Alzheimer’s could change how we treat both
item.description: The idea that two conditions at opposite ends of life might be biologically linked is beginning to upend long-standing assumptions in brain science, blurring a divide that has shaped the field.

item.title: What the looming sale of CNN means for Trump’s feud with the network
item.description: Animosity from the White House has taken on new meaning amid the network’s imminent sale to David Ellison’s Paramount Skydance, fueling anxiety among journalists.

item.title: Trump’s profane post threatening Iran’s power plants underscores tensions over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Gas prices have risen sharply as Iran limits the flow of oil through the Strait of Hormuz, ramping up the political pressure on President Donald Trump to end the conflict he started. His threat on Sunday heightens earlier warnings that he plans to inflict severe damage if there is no movement toward a deal.

item.title: We may have even less control over how long we live than previously thought
item.description: A study resets the longevity discussion, after previous research focused on those born between 1870 and 1900, and shows genetics and environment play roughly equal roles.

item.title: Easter destroys the ultimate weapon of tyranny
item.description: In proclaiming the resurrection of Jesus, Easter denies the power of death, Robert Barron, the bishop of the Diocese of Winona-Rochester and the founder of Word on Fire Catholic Ministries, writes.

item.title: Pope issues commanding call for peace as Trump, aides invoke God for war
item.description: “Let those who have weapons lay them down!” the first American pope declared, as the White House’s war in Iran and nativist agenda at home is testing the Vatican.

item.title: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now
item.description: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now
